title: The Decay
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:The Decay}}
The DecayThe Decay
==Notice== This page will likely be updated as the phenomenon worsens.
'''The Decay''' is an ongoing and Backrooms-wide phenomenon that began sometime around the beginning of 2076. It is predicted to have devastating consequences on the entirety of the Backrooms in the years to come. {{Blurimage|file=Early Decay.jpeg|width=400px|align=center|caption=A recent image of [[Level 0]]. As one can see, many of the level's light fixtures have begun to fail and falling particles have accumulated into a haze.|hovertext=Deterioration}} '''Description''''''Description''' Initially manifesting in ways that only posed a minor inconvenience at most, such as occasionally flickering lights and slightly faded sections of wallpaper, the '''Decay''' was suspected to be a largely harmless occurrence. However, over the course of years, the phenomenon has become more severe and is continuing to do so at a slowly increasing rate. Presently, some of its effects include parts of structures growing weaker, entire areas of skies dimming, and, rarely, small parts of levels collapsing entirely, which often leaves pits that lead straight to [[The Void]] (these have quickly become widely known as 'Void Pits' or 'Void Gaps'). In aquatic levels, this frequently creates whirlpools that spiral into these gaps, commonly causing even more of a disturbance than they otherwise would. Entities, objects, and even entire phenomena are also vulnerable to the '''Decay'''. Many entities have grown noticeably weaker, often needing to feed more often in order to survive. Some reports also describe them as acting fatigued and overly tired. Extremely rarely, parts of entities have even started to decay and break away, either taking the form of necrosis, severe weakening of adjacent body parts, or outright dissipation of bodily matter — corporeal or otherwise. Meanwhile, the effects of most phenomena and other anomalies have also marginally decreased to varying degrees. However, most dangerous entities and phenomena still pose a threat, even if slightly lessened. At the ever-increasing speed at which the '''Decay''' is progressing, it is likely that collapsing sections of levels will have become a common hazard in approximately two years, destroying the majority of colonies and outposts and killing large numbers of people in the process. Entities as a whole will pose significantly less of a threat, often fully decaying entirely, and severe weakening of certain phenomena, while beneficial in certain cases, will disrupt the normal state of levels that overly rely on them. Around a year after that, the entire Backrooms and its resident entities will have been reduced to nothingness. Many wanderers — particularly those who believe [[The Kauer Thesis]] — suspect that the sole reason the Backrooms is decaying is due to the tragic [[News Report|nuclear holocaust]] that wiped out the vast majority — if not all — of humanity in [[The Frontrooms]] decades ago. The reasoning for this states that, not only was the Backrooms formed from some aspect of collective human perception of the world around them, but it also needs that same aspect to continue existing. Hypothetically, the minds of humans in the Backrooms itself do not have the same effect and the dimension can only sustain itself for so long without anything supporting its existence. By extension, if a small part of humanity on Earth did manage to survive for a considerable amount of time after the war, that could explain why the Backrooms has survived for even this long. Many groups are working to find any possible solution to surviving the '''Decay''', though few have either dismissed the threat as something that will eventually go away on its own or dissolved into panicked chaos. Wanderers are advised to remain in their own bases, outposts, or colonies as much as possible for a better chance at safety in the likely event the '''Decay''' continues to worsen. '''God save us.'''
